title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy Korean seafood pancake, at home. This easy recipe brings the delicious flavors of Korea to your kitchen. Perfect for a savory snack or appetizer!
lowCompetitionKeywords: Korean green onion pancake with seafood
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon: Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
h2Headings: Step-by-Step: How to Make Haemul Pajeon
Tips for a Perfectly Crispy Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Haemul Pajeon, a traditional Korean seafood pancake, served with a savory dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the savory delight of homemade Haemul Pajeon, a classic Korean seafood and green onion pancake.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, packed with a delightful medley of fresh seafood and crisp green onions. Its distinct crispy edges and soft, flavorful interior make it an irresistible appetizer or snack. This traditional dish is perfect for sharing, offering a taste of authentic Korean comfort food.

ingredients.notes: or a mix of all-purpose and rice flour
shrimp, squid, mussels, oysters, cut into bite-sized pieces
for frying, or more as needed

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, salt, and cold water until smooth. Stir in the beaten egg. The batter should be slightly thick, similar to pancake batter. Avoid overmixing to prevent a tough pancake.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean and pat dry your mixed seafood. If using larger pieces, cut them into bite-sized portions. Wash and trim the green onions, cutting them into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and red chili pepper (if using).

instructions.title: Make the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, minced garlic, and toasted sesame seeds. Stir well and set aside. This sauce adds a perfect tangy complement to the savory pancake.

instructions.title: Assemble the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add about 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil. Once hot, pour about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of batter into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a thin circle. Arrange a generous amount of green onions, sliced yellow onion, and mixed seafood on top of the batter. Lightly drizzle a bit more batter over the toppings to help them stick.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancake
instructions.text: Cook for 5-7 minutes on one side, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pancake. Carefully flip the pancake and cook for another 5-7 minutes until the other side is golden and the seafood is cooked through. You can press down gently with a spatula to ensure even cooking and crispiness.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, transfer the Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Garnish with thinly sliced red chili pepper, if desired. Cut the pancake into wedges or squares and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce. Repeat for remaining batter and ingredients.

tips: For an extra crispy pancake, use very cold water for the batter and add a tablespoon of cornstarch. Also, ensure your pan is hot and use enough oil.
Feel free to customize your seafood mix. Squid, shrimp, mussels, and oysters are common, but you can also add clams or scallops. Ensure all seafood is fresh and thoroughly cleaned.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its optimal crispiness. It pairs wonderfully with a side of kimchi or other Korean banchan (side dishes).
article:
Discover the culinary delight that is Haemul Pajeon, a quintessential Korean seafood pancake that has captivated food lovers worldwide. With its irresistible crispy edges, tender interior, and generous scattering of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions, Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's an experience. Whether you’re a seasoned fan of Korean cuisine or just beginning your flavorful journey, learning to make this savory pancake at home is a rewarding endeavor. Prepare to impress your taste buds and your guests with this authentic and incredibly satisfying treat.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

Haemul Pajeon (해물파전) literally translates to "seafood scallion pancake." It's a beloved Korean savory pancake characterized by its substantial amount of fresh green onions (scallions) and an assortment of seafood, all bound together in a light, crispy batter. Unlike some thinner, crepe-like pancakes, pajeon boasts a satisfying chewiness on the inside and a golden, often slightly irregular, crispy exterior that's simply addictive. In Korea, it's a popular anju (food served with alcohol), especially cherished on rainy days when its sizzling sound and comforting warmth perfectly complement a glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or soju. Its origins are deeply rooted in Korean home cooking, evolving from simple scallion pancakes to the seafood-rich version we adore today, often enjoyed as an appetizer, snack, or even a light meal.

## Ingredients for Authentic Haemul Pajeon

Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon begins with selecting fresh, quality ingredients. The beauty of this dish lies in its simplicity, allowing the natural flavors of the seafood and scallions to shine.

**For the Pancake:**
*   **Green Onions (Scallions):** 1 large bunch (about 10-15 stalks), trimmed and cut into 2-3 inch pieces. These are the star alongside the seafood.
*   **Seafood:** 1 cup mixed seafood (e.g., shrimp, squid rings or calamari, mussels, oysters). Ensure seafood is fresh or thoroughly thawed and patted dry. Small bay scallops are also excellent.
*   **All-Purpose Flour:** 1 cup.
*   **Cornstarch or Rice Flour:** 2 tablespoons. This is key for that extra crispiness.
*   **Cold Water or Anchovy Broth:** 1 cup. Using cold liquid helps create a crispier texture. Anchovy broth adds a deeper umami flavor.
*   **Egg:** 1 large, lightly beaten (for the batter, and sometimes a second one for drizzling on top during cooking).
*   **Garlic:** 1-2 cloves, minced (optional, for the batter).
*   **Salt:** ½ teaspoon, or to taste.
*   **Black Pepper:** ¼ teaspoon, or to taste.
*   **Vegetable Oil:** For frying, plenty of it!

**For the Dipping Sauce (Yangnyeom Ganjang):**
*   **Soy Sauce:** 2 tablespoons
*   **Rice Vinegar:** 1 tablespoon
*   **Water:** 1 tablespoon
*   **Gochugaru (Korean Chili Flakes):** ½ teaspoon (optional, for a kick)
*   **Sesame Oil:** ½ teaspoon
*   **Toasted Sesame Seeds:** ½ teaspoon
*   **Minced Green Onion:** 1 teaspoon (from the white part)

## Step-by-Step: How to Make Haemul Pajeon

Follow this straightforward Haemul Pajeon Recipe to create a masterpiece in your own kitchen.

1.  **Prepare the Seafood and Green Onions:** If using frozen seafood, thaw it completely and pat dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture. Cut any larger pieces of seafood (like shrimp or squid) into bite-sized portions. Wash and trim the green onions, then cut them into 2-3 inch lengths.
2.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch (or rice flour), salt, and black pepper. Gradually add the cold water (or broth) while whisking continuously until you have a smooth, lump-free batter. Stir in the beaten egg and minced garlic (if using). The batter should be thick enough to coat the back of a spoon but still pourable.
3.  **Combine Ingredients:** Add the prepared green onions and seafood to the batter. Gently fold them in until they are evenly coated. Try not to overmix.
4.  **Prepare the Dipping Sauce:** While the batter rests for a few minutes, combine all the dipping sauce ingredients in a small bowl. Stir well and set aside.
5.  **Cook the Pancake:** Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring the entire surface of the pan is coated. The oil should be shimmering hot.
6.  **Form the Pancake:** Pour about half of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it out evenly with a spatula to form a round pancake about ½ inch thick. Arrange any sparse areas with more green onions or seafood so they are evenly distributed.
7.  **Cook Until Golden and Crispy:** Cook for 5-7 minutes on the first side, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the bottom is set. You might see some bubbles forming.
8.  **Flip and Finish:** Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pan to help crisp up the second side. Cook for another 4-6 minutes, pressing down gently with the spatula to ensure even cooking and contact with the pan, until the second side is also golden brown and the seafood is cooked through.
9.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Repeat with the remaining batter for a second pancake. Cut the pancakes into wedges or squares and serve immediately with the dipping sauce.

## Tips for a Perfectly Crispy Korean Seafood Pancake

Achieving that coveted crispy texture is key to a truly authentic Haemul Pajeon. Here are some essential tips for your Haemul Pajeon Recipe:

*   **Cold Batter is Best:** Use very cold water or broth for your batter. The temperature difference between the cold batter and hot oil helps create a crispier exterior.
*   **Don't Skimp on Oil:** Pajeon is not a low-fat dish. Generous amounts of oil are crucial for deep-frying the surface and achieving that desirable crispiness.
*   **Hot Pan is Essential:** Make sure your skillet is screaming hot before adding the batter. A sufficiently hot pan prevents the pancake from sticking and ensures immediate crisping.
*   **Thin Batter, Not Thick:** While the batter needs to hold the ingredients together, it shouldn't be too thick. A thinner batter allows for more surface contact with the hot oil, leading to a crispier result. The combination of all-purpose flour and a touch of cornstarch or rice flour also contributes significantly to crispiness.
*   **Even Distribution:** Take the time to spread the green onions and seafood evenly in the pan. This ensures every bite has a good mix of flavors and textures.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pan:** Cook one pancake at a time, especially if your pan isn't exceptionally large. Overcrowding lowers the pan's temperature and results in soggy pancakes.
*   **Press Down Gently:** After flipping, gently press the pancake with your spatula. This helps ensure good contact with the hot pan and promotes even browning and crispiness.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan. As it cools, it will lose some of its crispiness.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile. It shines as an appetizer, a satisfying snack, or even a light lunch or dinner. Traditionally, it's cut into bite-sized wedges or squares and served hot with a simple yet flavorful dipping sauce.

The classic dipping sauce (yangnyeom ganjang) outlined in the ingredients section is a perfect accompaniment. Its balance of savory soy, tangy vinegar, and a hint of sesame complements the rich flavors of the pancake without overpowering them. Feel free to adjust the gochugaru to your spice preference.

Beyond the sauce, the quintessential pairing for Haemul Pajeon, especially on a rainy day, is a bottle of chilled makgeolli. The slightly sweet, effervescent rice wine cuts through the richness of the pancake beautifully. Soju or a cold beer are also excellent choices, making Haemul Pajeon a fantastic dish for social gatherings.

## Variations and Frequently Asked Questions

The world of Korean pancakes offers exciting variations, and Haemul Pajeon is no exception.

**Variations:**
*   **Kimchi Pajeon:** For a spicy, tangy kick, add chopped kimchi to your batter.
*   **Buchu Pajeon:** Replace some or all of the green onions with Korean chives (buchu) for a milder, distinctly aromatic flavor.
*   **Vegetable Pajeon:** If seafood isn't your preference, load it up with thinly sliced carrots, onions, mushrooms, or bell peppers.
*   **Specific Seafood Pajeon:** Focus on one type of seafood, such as Oyster Pajeon (Gul Pajeon) or Shrimp Pajeon (Saewoo Pajeon).

**Frequently Asked Questions:**

*   **Can I make Haemul Pajeon gluten-free?** Yes! Simply substitute the all-purpose flour with a gluten-free all-purpose flour blend. Ensure your cornstarch or rice flour is also certified gluten-free.
*   **Can I prepare the batter ahead of time?** You can prepare the dry ingredients and mix them with the wet ingredients (water, egg) a few hours in advance and store it in the fridge. However, it's best to add the fresh seafood and green onions just before cooking to prevent them from becoming soggy or losing their texture.
*   **What if I don't have fresh seafood?** Good quality frozen mixed seafood works perfectly fine. Just make sure to thaw it completely and pat it very dry to prevent excess water from making your batter watery.
*   **How do I reheat leftover Haemul Pajeon?** The best way to regain some crispiness is to reheat it in a dry skillet over medium heat, or in an oven or air fryer at 350°F (175°C) until heated through and crispy. Microwaving will make it soft and chewy.
*   **Is Haemul Pajeon spicy?** Traditionally, Haemul Pajeon itself is not spicy, allowing the natural flavors of the seafood and scallions to shine. Any spiciness comes from the optional gochugaru in the dipping sauce.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a Korean seafood pancake; it's a testament to the country's rich culinary heritage, offering a delightful balance of textures and flavors. Whether you follow this detailed Haemul Pajeon Recipe for your first attempt or customize it with your favorite ingredients, you're in for a truly satisfying and delicious experience. So, gather your ingredients, fire up your skillet, and enjoy the comforting, crispy goodness of homemade Haemul Pajeon!
